name: Codex Harness Foundry
goal: Provide a reusable Codex collaboration harness with agent context composition, milestone planning, and verification workflows.
stack: Node.js 22, TypeScript, pnpm, PowerShell, and agents-md
description: Codex Harness Foundry: Provide a reusable Codex collaboration harness with agent context composition, milestone planning, and verification workflows.
targetUsers: Builders who want Codex to operate from repository state with planner, builder, and verifier roles.
successMetric: A new repository can be forked, initialized, and verified in a few minutes.
mvpBoundary: Shared agent context, milestone planning, task-board orchestration, verification, and bootstrap automation for derived projects.

autonomy.basePrompt: Act as the leader/orchestrator. Read repository truth, request planner publication with `pnpm planner:propose` when no ready tasks exist, inspect `planning/planner-output.json`, and accept it with `pnpm planner:publish` when appropriate. If the active final milestone is fully verified and there is no later milestone blueprint, request `pnpm next-milestone:propose`, inspect `planning/next-milestone-output.json`, and accept it with `pnpm next-milestone:publish` when appropriate. Then advance the highest-leverage published task and leave the repo in a verifiable state.
autonomy.resumePrompt: Resume as the leader/orchestrator from repository state and the previous Codex thread. If no ready tasks exist, request planner output with `pnpm planner:propose`, inspect `planning/planner-output.json`, and publish only after accepting the proposal. If the active final milestone is fully verified and there is no later milestone blueprint, request `pnpm next-milestone:propose`, inspect `planning/next-milestone-output.json`, and publish only after accepting that proposal. Then advance the next meaningful published task and stop after a coherent, verifiable unit of work.
